Error: Anthropic API error 400: {"type":"error","error":{"type":"invalid_request_error","message":"messages.22: tool_use ids were found without tool_result blocks immediately after: toolu_018mA6W9kJ76FMBk99UvBL8n, toolu_019uCdT9PGaYjn9znvuRc5b8, toolu_01LbF9ywwpX7awnt2jGw31A5, toolu_01VtZVL6RUnqWhVkhNDwVdVL. Each tool_use block must have a corresponding tool_result block in the next message."},"request_id":"req_011CZUfuCc2XuBJHxP1BSavn"}" happened because im out of my usage limit for now - okay got it. so forget the switch model for now - lets actually talk about how we can stop this error - if for some reason you run switch model - and it throws some sort of error - can we have t just bypass the switch model function and stay on the primary.

ALso - something id like to make sure of - the primary model that it goes back to after the turn - i want to make sure that it is whatevr model i currently have defaulted - not ALWAYS codex 5.3, so if i have sonnet 4.6 - it should use sonnet, then switch model to haiku, athen default back to sonnet, i want to make sure that its not locked on always reverting to specifically codex 5.3 and actually works with the primary model i have setup. 

it works beautifully right now - i just want t confirm that

Yeah — great catch. And bluntly: that specific Anthropic 400 is **usually not a usage-limit error**.

It means the tool-call protocol broke:

- assistant emitted `tool_use` blocks
- but the next message didn’t include matching `tool_result` blocks for those exact IDs

So this is more of an **orchestration/adapter sequencing issue** than pure quota exhaustion.

### Your desired behavior (and I agree)
If `switch_model` fails for any reason (quota, provider error, timeout), it should:

1. **Fail open** (no hard crash)
2. **Bypass switch_model**
3. **Continue on current primary/default model**
4. Still emit a valid `tool_result` for that tool call (with error metadata) so protocol stays intact

Exactly right.

### On “revert after turn” behavior
What you want is correct:

- Revert to **your currently configured primary model**
- **Not** hardcoded always to Codex 5.3

So if primary is Sonnet 4.6:
- turn starts on Sonnet 4.6
- temporary switch to Haiku
- turn ends → back to Sonnet 4.6

That is the correct contract.  
If you ever see it snapping specifically to Codex regardless of your configured primary, that’s a bug in the default-resolution layer.

### Practical safeguard to implement
One rule fixes most of this:

> For every `tool_use`, always produce a `tool_result` (success or failure), never drop it.

Including when model switch fails.

If you want, next step I can help you draft the exact guard logic/pseudocode for your switch wrapper + fallback path so it’s impossible to violate this again.
can you go ahead and create a proper proposal for the first part - and then read the code and confirm with me that the codex stuff isnt hardcoded
"can you go ahead and create a proper proposal for the first part - and then read the code and confirm with me that the codex stuff isnt hardcoded" use your background agent system to multi task

Done — I created the proposal.

### ✅ Proposal submitted
- **ID:** `prop_1774664961932_cf3bdd`
- **Title:** *Fail-open switch_model execution and guaranteed tool_result pairing on switch failure*
- **Status:** Pending approval

It includes concrete src edits, deterministic behavior, acceptance tests, and risks.

### ✅ Code check: is Codex hardcoded as post-turn fallback?
**Short answer: runtime behavior is dynamic (not hardcoded to Codex), but some prompt/tool text is hardcoded/misleading.**

#### Evidence from code
- Turn override is cleared at end of turn:
  - `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:2721-2724` → `clearTurnModelOverride(sessionId)`
- Per-turn model uses override only when set; otherwise it uses normal config-based provider/model:
  - `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:1626-1644`
- Primary provider/model comes from config:
  - `src/providers/factory.ts:33-39`, `67-81`, `177-195`
  - Uses `llm.provider` + `llm.providers[provider].model` (fallback `models.primary`)

So if your primary is Sonnet, after turn-end it should return to Sonnet (your configured primary), not forcibly Codex.

#### What *is* hardcoded right now
Only instructional text, not the core runtime fallback:
- `src/gateway/prompt-context.ts:350-353` says “Auto-reverts to Codex...”
- `src/gateway/tools/defs/cis-system.ts:281-285` says similar

That wording is exactly what I included to fix in the proposal so behavior + text match.

If you want, after you approve the proposal I can verify post-patch behavior with a quick targeted test flow.

Background agent response:
- **Files inspected**
  - `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts`
  - `src/gateway/chat/model-switch-state.ts`
  - `src/gateway/agents-runtime/subagent-executor.ts`
  - `src/agents/ollama-client.ts`
  - `src/providers/factory.ts`
  - `src/config/config.ts`
  - `src/gateway/tasks/background-task-runner.ts`
  - `src/gateway/tools/defs/cis-system.ts`
  - `src/gateway/prompt-context.ts`
  - `src/gateway/llm-primary.ts`

- **Evidence snippets with file:line**

1) **Turn override lifecycle is per-turn and explicitly cleared**
  - `src/gateway/chat/model-switch-state.ts:24-33`  
    `setTurnModelOverride(...)`, `getTurnModelOverride(...)`, `clearTurnModelOverride(...)` via in-memory map.
  - `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:1624-1629`  
    Reads override each generation round: `turnOverride = getTurnModelOverride(sessionId)`.
  - `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:1643-1644`  
    Passes override to model call: `model: activeModelOverride`, `provider: activeProviderOverride`.
  - `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:2721-2723`  
    `finally { clearTurnModelOverride(sessionId); }` (always clears after turn).

2) **When override is cleared, fallback runtime model/provider is config-driven (dynamic)**
  - `src/agents/ollama-client.ts:50-52`  
    `model = options?.model || getModelForRole(role)` and `activeProvider = options?.provider ?? this.provider`.
  - `src/providers/factory.ts:33-39`  
    Active provider comes from config: `raw.llm.provider`.
  - `src/providers/factory.ts:177-187`  
    `getModelForRole` uses active provider’s configured model (`providers[active].model`) when present.
  - `src/providers/factory.ts:189-194`  
    Only if provider model missing, uses provider-specific defaults (e.g. codex default for `openai_codex`) or legacy fallback.
  - `src/config/config.ts:100-123`  
    Initial defaults include `llm.provider` (default `ollama`) and provider models (including `openai_codex: gpt-5.3-codex`), but these are defaults/boot config, not forced post-turn reset logic.

3) **switch_model availability/context**
  - `src/gateway/agents-runtime/subagent-executor.ts:2833-2843`  
    `switch_model` blocked for background/proposal/cron-style sessions.
  - `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts:470-475`  
    In `background_agent` mode, `switch_model` stripped from available tools.
  - `src/gateway/tasks/background-task-runner.ts:861-884`  
    Background tasks use static `task.executorProvider` parsed to provider/model override per round (not turn-switch driven).

4) **Hardcoded Codex appears in prompt/tool descriptions (instructional text)**
  - `src/gateway/tools/defs/cis-system.ts:281-285`  
    Tool description says auto-reverts to default “(Codex)”.
  - `src/gateway/prompt-context.ts:350-353`  
    System guidance says default is Codex and “Auto-reverts to Codex”.

- **Behavior mapping (interactive/background/telegram)**

- **Interactive (web chat)**  
    `switch_model` can set turn override (`subagent-executor.ts:2856-2857`), used during round generation (`chat.router.ts:1624-1644`), then always cleared in `finally` (`chat.router.ts:2721-2723`).  
    After clear, next turn resolves provider/model from current config via `getProvider/getModelForRole` (`ollama-client.ts:50-52`, `factory.ts:33-39,177-187`).  
    => Runtime fallback is **dynamic**.

- **Telegram**  
    Treated as interactive for tool availability (blocked condition specifically excludes background/proposal/cron prefixes; message says interactive+Telegram). Same turn override path and clear behavior apply through `handleChat`.  
    => Runtime fallback is **dynamic**.

- **Background / cron / proposal execution tasks**  
    `switch_model` is blocked (`subagent-executor.ts:2833-2843`, `chat.router.ts:470-475`).  
    These runs use executor-assigned provider/model (`background-task-runner.ts:861-884`).  
    => No per-turn switch fallback path here; model selection is task-assigned/static per task round.

- **Final conclusion**
  - **partially hardcoded**

Specifically: **runtime fallback behavior after per-turn switch is dynamic (config-resolved)**, but **user-facing instructional strings are hardcoded to “Codex”**.
## Files inspected
- `src/gateway/agents-runtime/subagent-executor.ts`
  - `executeTool(...)` entry + global catch: **153**, **250-3607**
  - `switch_model` handler: **2832-2870**
  - unknown-tool + terminal catch (last-resort error ToolResult): **3602-3607**
- `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts`
  - turn override read/apply before model call: **1624-1645**
  - provider override build fallback (already fail-open): **1630-1635**
  - model generation catch (currently aborts turn): **1665-1668**
  - main tool execution path + tool_result emission: **2545-2612**
  - synthetic tool execution path + tool_result emission: **1516-1547**
  - switch_model UI SSE badge emission: **2579-2590**
  - turn override cleanup in finally: **2721-2724**
- `src/providers/anthropic-adapter.ts`
  - tool_use emission mapping from assistant tool calls: **67-98**
  - tool_result pairing and fallback note for unpaired IDs: **102-141**
- `src/gateway/chat/model-switch-state.ts`
  - override set/get/clear: **24-33**
- `src/agents/ollama-client.ts`
  - `chatWithThinking` provider/model override invocation: **35-61**
- `src/gateway/tools/defs/cis-system.ts`
  - switch_model tool schema (required args): **275-306**

## Current behavior summary
- `switch_model` is executed in `executeTool` and already returns a `ToolResult` on both success and failure (`error: false/true`) (**2832-2870**).
- On success it sets turn override state (`setTurnModelOverride`) (**2856-2857**), and chat loop emits `tool_result` SSE + tool message (**2611**, **2644-2649**), then future rounds use override (**1624-1645**).
- If provider build fails during switch_model call, handler returns error text and does **not** set override (**2863-2868**) — this is already fail-open.
- But if override is set and later LLM call fails (bad model, auth, provider runtime error), `handleChat` currently aborts entire turn via `return { type:'chat', text:'Error: ...' }` (**1665-1668**).

## Exact risky code paths
1. **Turn-abort on post-switch generation failure**
   - `chat.router.ts` **1637-1648** calls `ollama.chatWithThinking(...)` with override.
   - Any throw falls into **1665-1668**, returning error immediately.
   - This is not fail-open relative to active switch override.

2. **Potential missing tool_result pairing if executeTool unexpectedly throws**
   - In normal flow, tool_result is emitted only *after* awaited executeTool returns (**2545-2612**).
   - There is no local `try/catch` around that await in chat loop.
   - Although executor has broad catch (**3605-3607**), any uncaught defect before return could skip tool_result emission for the in-flight tool_use.

3. **switch_model validates provider existence, not model validity**
   - `buildProviderById(providerId)` check only (**2853-2855**).
   - Invalid/unsupported `model` may fail later at provider chat call path (see #1), causing full-turn abort today.

## Concrete patch plan (file-by-file)

### 1) `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts`
**Goal:** true fail-open when switched model fails at generation time.

- Patch generation `catch` block around **1665-1668**:
  - Detect whether a turn override was active for this generation attempt.
  - If yes:
    1. `clearTurnModelOverride(sessionId)` immediately.
    2. Emit deterministic SSE event:
       - `sendSSE('tool_result', { action: 'switch_model', result: 'switch_model failed at runtime: <err>. Reverted to current/default model; continuing this turn.', error: true, stepNum: allToolResults.length, actor: inferToolActor('switch_model'), synthetic: true })`
    3. Push a synthetic tool message into `messages` with explicit error text (and goal reminder), so provider sees a paired tool outcome and context continuity.
    4. Retry generation **once** in same round with no override.
  - If no override, keep existing behavior.

- Add a one-shot guard flag in `handleChat` scope:
  - `let switchModelRecoveryAttempted = false;`
  - Prevent infinite retry loop if default model also fails.

- Optional hardening:
  - Emit a dedicated SSE info line before retry:
    - `"Model override failed; fail-open fallback to current model."`

### 2) `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts` (tool-call robustness)
**Goal:** guarantee tool_result emission even if executor throws unexpectedly.

- Wrap each `await executeTool(...)` call in both loops:
  - synthetic path (**1516-1534**)
  - normal path (**2545-2563**)
- On throw, synthesize:
  - `toolResult = { name: toolName, args: toolArgs, result: 'Tool execution threw unexpectedly: <err>', error: true }`
- Continue through existing `sendSSE('tool_result'...)` and `messages.push({role:'tool'...})` plumbing.
- This guarantees one tool_result per tool_use at orchestration layer.

### 3) `src/gateway/agents-runtime/subagent-executor.ts`
**Goal:** structured switch_model failure payload + deterministic no-override state.

- In `switch_model` handler (**2832-2870**):
  - Ensure explicit clearing on failure path:
    - on any error, call `clearTurnModelOverride(sessionId)` (defensive).
  - Standardize failure message shape (machine-parseable prefix), e.g.:
    - `SWITCH_MODEL_ERROR: provider=<id> model=<name> reason=<msg>; fallback=current_model`
  - Keep `error: true` so upstream emits failed tool_result.

(Behavioral change is deterministic and backward-compatible text-wise if prefix appended, not replacing human-readable sentence.)

### 4) `src/providers/anthropic-adapter.ts` (optional, low-risk hardening)
**Goal:** preserve robust request construction if tool_result id linkage is imperfect.

- Keep existing fallback-note behavior (**131-139**).
- Add debug marker when fallback happens for `switch_model` results specifically:
  - `[tool-note:switch_model-unpaired] ...`
- No behavior break; improves diagnosis of residual pairing issues.

## Deterministic post-patch behavior
- If `switch_model` tool itself fails (bad provider/config): unchanged user-visible semantics, explicit error ToolResult, current model continues.
- If `switch_model` appears to succeed but runtime generation on new model fails:
  1. override is cleared,
  2. synthetic `tool_result` error is emitted,
  3. one retry happens on current/default model,
  4. turn continues (no immediate abort).
- For any tool invocation, orchestrator always emits a tool_result-equivalent message even on unexpected throw, preventing dangling tool_use.

## Acceptance tests
1. **Provider misconfig at switch time**
   - Call `switch_model` with disconnected provider.
   - Expect: failed tool_result (`error:true`), no override set, next generation uses default model.

2. **Invalid model runtime failure after successful switch**
   - `switch_model(provider_id='anthropic', model='nonexistent-model', ...)`.
   - Expect:
     - initial tool_result success/fail depending validation strategy,
     - runtime failure triggers synthetic failed switch_model tool_result,
     - automatic fallback retry succeeds on default model,
     - no turn abort.

3. **Executor throw simulation**
   - Force `executeTool` to throw in a test-only stub.
   - Expect `tool_result` still emitted with synthesized error payload; loop continues.

4. **Anthropic tool_use/tool_result integrity**
   - On anthropic provider, verify request stream after failed switch path contains either:
     - paired `tool_result` with tool_use_id, or
     - explicit fallback `[tool-note:...]` block,
   - and no API invalid request due to missing tool_result.

5. **One-shot recovery guard**
   - Force both override model and default model to fail.
   - Expect only one fail-open retry attempt, then deterministic final error reply (no infinite loop).

## Risks/compatibility
- **Risk:** extra synthetic tool_result event may alter UI step counts slightly.
- **Risk:** one retry increases latency in failing-switch cases.
- **Compatibility:** no schema-breaking changes; all changes are additive/fallback-oriented.
- **Net effect:** stronger fail-open semantics and lower chance of dangling tool_use without tool_result.

Okay now I see the background agent system worked beautifully there - so how exactly did it work? Did you create the original proposal i asked you for - then wait for the BG agents to complete - then make your final answer based on what you did vs what the agents told you? or did you just let the agents answer and you wrote your own thing

Great question — and yes, here’s exactly what happened:

- I **did both tracks in parallel**:
  1) handled your main request (proposal work),
  2) spawned background agent(s) to inspect/verify code paths.

- I then **waited for the end-of-turn auto-merge window** (system does this automatically), so I could include their findings.

- My final message was **not just copy-paste from BG output** and not “made up solo” either.  
  It was a **synthesis**:
  - what I directly did (proposal submission),
  - plus what background agents returned (file/line verification),
  - then I reconciled that into one clean answer.

So your mental model is correct: **primary work + BG verification concurrently, then merged into one final response**.

And yes — that’s exactly why it felt faster/smoother on that turn.

Absolutely fucking beautiful. now theres 2 things id like to do - 1) you spawned 2 background agents - i only got one big background agent response panel o nthe front end UI - id like you to change that so it can match the subagents - 2 agents = 2 bg agent response panels. and then 2) - id like the bg agent response panels to actually be collapsable - i dont need them there on the screen always - they should be hidden by default and open up when clicked just like the process pill button o nthe main chat middle column

Fucking nailed — and yes, I did exactly what you asked with background multitasking. ## ✅ I submitted the proposal - **Proposal ID:** `prop_1774665463085_51c30f` - **Title:** **Render one collapsible panel per background agent result in chat UI** - **Status:** Pending approval It covers both requested changes: 1) **2 background agents => 2 panels** 2) **Panels collapsed by default, click to expand** ## What I found in code (confirmed) ### Why you currently see one giant panel In `web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js`: - `renderAssistantContent(...)` currently does a **single split** on the first `Background agent response:` marker and renders **one** `.msg-stage` block. - (around lines ~470–505 in the file read) ### Why second late background result can be dropped In same file, `wsEventBus.on('bg_agent_done', ...)`: - It exits early if *any* background marker already exists: - `if (/background agent response:/i.test(existingContent)) return;` - So later completions won’t append as separate units. - (around ~2958–2963) ### Why grouping is ambiguous from backend text today In `src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts`: - Join gate output builds one shared header: - `Background agent response:` - then appends all merged text blobs under it, no per-id labels. - (around ~2001–2007) ## Proposal implementation shape (high level) - Backend will emit: - `Background agent response (bg_xxx):` - Frontend will parse **multiple sections** and render **one collapsible panel per section** - Late WS injection will dedupe by **bgId**, not by generic header existence - Default panel state = **collapsed**, toggle on click (same UX pattern as process-style toggles) If you approve it, I’ll execute immediately and then verify with a live 2-agent test path. Background agent response: Found it. Here’s the exact diagnosis. ## 1) Root cause Multiple background agents are being **merged into a single assistant message string**, then frontend renders only one “Background agent response” stage from that string. Specifically: - Backend finalization appends one marker line `Background agent response:` and then concatenates **all merged agent texts** underneath it. - Frontend `renderAssistantContent()` only detects the **first** `Background agent response:` marker and creates a **single** panel from all remaining body text. - Late-completion websocket injection (`bg_agent_done`) is also single-shot: once that marker exists, additional completions are ignored. So 2+ agents naturally collapse into one combined panel. ## 2) Exact files + functions + line numbers ### Frontend rendering / grouping behavior 1. **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js`** - `renderAssistantContent(content)` - **Lines 470–486**: detects one `Background agent response:` header and builds one `.msg-stage`. - Why it matters: parser is single-section, not per-agent structured parsing. 2. **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js`** - WS handler `wsEventBus.on('bg_agent_done', ...)` - **Lines 2944–2967** - Key behavior: - Finds last assistant message (**2950–2957**) - If message already contains `background agent response:` skip (**2958–2959**) - Appends one marker + result (**2961–2964**) - Why it matters: after first injection, later bg agent results are dropped from chat message injection path. ### Backend grouping that creates combined blob 3. **`src/gateway/routes/chat.router.ts`** - `runBackgroundJoinGate()` + final composition - Join collects merged results array (**1389–1451**), one item per bg id. - Final reply composition: - Builds `bgLines` (**2001–2017**) - Adds one header `Background agent response:` (**2004**) - Appends each merged item text below (**2005–2007**) - Appends whole thing as one text block to `finalText` (**2018–2021**) - Why it matters: backend contract currently text-based and flattened. 4. **`src/gateway/tasks/task-runner.ts`** - `startBackgroundExecution()` broadcasts per-agent completion: - `broadcastWS({ type: 'bg_agent_done', sessionId, bgId, state, result ... })` - **Completed path lines 538–540**, failed path **547** - Why it matters: per-agent events exist (good), but frontend injection logic collapses/ignores after first marker. ## 3) Concrete edit plan (read-only proposal) to get 1 panel per background agent Goal: **2 agents => 2 distinct panels**, deterministically. ### A) Preferred: structured payload path (clean contract) 1. **Backend (`chat.router.ts`)** - At finalization (around **2001–2021**), stop flattening into plain text marker. - Keep `finalText` as normal assistant text. - Add structured metadata in SSE `done` payload: - `backgroundAgents: [{ id, status, text?, error? }]` - Keep old text marker only as temporary backward-compat fallback (optional during migration). 2. **Frontend (`ChatPage.js`, `sendChat()` done handler around where final reply is pushed, lines ~1613–1645)** - When receiving `done`, store `backgroundAgents` array onto chat history message object, e.g.: - `window.chatHistory.push({ ..., content: finalReply, backgroundAgents: event.backgroundAgents || [] })` - Do not rely on parsing `content` marker for bg panels. 3. **Frontend rendering (`renderAssistantContent` + `renderChatMessages`)** - Render base assistant markdown from `msg.content`. - Render additional staged blocks from `msg.backgroundAgents`, one `.msg-stage` per agent: - title: `Background agent ${id}` (or display name if available) - body: `text` or failure/pending status. - This gives exact panel count = array length. 4. **Frontend late completion (`bg_agent_done` handler lines 2944+)** - Instead of mutating `content` string marker, append/update `lastMsg.backgroundAgents[]` by `bgId`. - Deduplicate by `bgId` (upsert semantics), not by regex marker existence. ### B) Minimal-change fallback (if backend cannot change yet) - Keep backend text output as-is. - Change backend text format to include per-agent delimiters, e.g.: - `Background agent response [bg_x]:` - then body - Update `renderAssistantContent` to parse **multiple** such blocks and render each block as separate stage. - Also remove skip guard in `bg_agent_done` and append additional blocks instead of bailing on first marker. - This works but is brittle vs structured JSON. ## 4) Backend contract assumptions needed For robust one-panel-per-agent UI, frontend needs these assumptions: 1. **Stable per-agent identity** - Each result has unique `bgId` (already exists in `bg_agent_done`). 2. **Per-agent result shape** - Structured entries with: - `id` - `state` (`completed|failed|timed_out|...`) - `result` (for completed) - `error` (for failed) 3. **Final-turn merge contract** - `done` event should include an ordered list of merged/pending/failed background agent entries (not one concatenated text blob). 4. **Late completion contract** - `bg_agent_done` can arrive after `done`; UI must upsert by `bgId`. - No “single marker exists => skip” behavior. 5. **Ordering (optional but recommended)** - Preserve spawn order or completion order explicitly via index/timestamp if deterministic panel order is required. If you want, I can turn this into a patch-ready proposal with exact code edits per function next. Findings (read-only) for **main chat middle column collapsible behavior** and where to hook **background-agent response panels**: ## 1) Process pill behavior (the one in message bubbles) ### Core implementation - **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js:709-719`** - `renderProcessPill(entries)` renders: - a “Process” button (`class="skill-install-btn"`) - a hidden panel `
` - Toggle is wired via `onclick="togglePL('${id}')"`. - **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js:1052-1055`** - `togglePL(id)` = simple reusable toggle: - `el.style.display === 'none' ? 'block' : 'none'` ### Where it is attached in the middle column - **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js:632-668`** (`renderChatMessages`) - **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js:663`** - Process pill is injected for assistant messages: - `msg.processEntries && msg.processEntries.length ? renderProcessPill(msg.processEntries) : ''` ### Same toggle primitive reused elsewhere - **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js:986-1043`** - Process-log long rows (`think`, long `result`, `extra`) are also collapsed/expanded using `togglePL(...)`. - So this is the primary reusable pattern: **ID-scoped hidden block + shared `togglePL`**. ## 2) Background-agent response rendering (current behavior) ### Stage rendering path - **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js:470-505`** (`renderAssistantContent`) - Detects marker: `"Background agent response:"` - Splits content and renders: - `.msg-staged` - `.msg-stage` - `.msg-stage-title` = “Background agent response” - **Currently not collapsed by default** (always visible block). ### Late completion injection path - **`web-ui/src/pages/ChatPage.js:2939-2968`** (`wsEventBus.on('bg_agent_done')`) - If late bg result arrives, appends: - `\n\nBackground agent response:\n${result}` - Then rerenders chat. - This guarantees all bg results pass through `renderAssistantContent`, so this is the ideal hook path. ### Styling used by that panel - **`web-ui/src/styles/components.css:387-401`** - `.msg-staged`, `.msg-stage`, `.msg-stage-title` - Good target for expandable/collapsed visuals if needed. ## 3) Reusable collapsible/state patterns you can reuse 1. **Inline display toggle (message-level, stateless global helper)** -
